digest.component_or_area: rebar config schema, event-signing seam, and reconciler inbound writer
propositions: Remove seven settled/retired config keys from the schema and hardcode their behavior always-on with no back-out branch or doc references remaining.
Activate verify.completion_floor_active in this project's rebar.toml since it is assessed as fully implemented and calibrated.

Stand up SSHSIG signing identity for every ticket-event writer (local dev, AWS review-bot/auto-lander, GitHub Actions reconciler/canary) via per-environment key-file delivery, with the require_authenticated flip out of scope.

Fix the reconciler inbound writer _write_event_file which bypasses the signing seam by stamping+signing author fields inline rather than routing through _seam.append_event, preserving deferred batch-commit orchestration.

Deliver the private signing key as a 0600 file path per environment (~/.ssh key locally, SSM SecureString materialized at boot for AWS, GitHub encrypted secret written to a temp file for GHA), never committed or logged.
Run a doc-accuracy sweep confirming no committed doc/help/release-note references any removed config key.
